behavior_summary: U8P_PLAQUEEW::look handles the player examining the plaque: it displays a short descriptive text ('plaque ') and then suspends/end the U8P_PLAQUEEW::look process so the player can read it.
U8P_PLAQUEEW::use delegates the 'U8P_PLAQUEEW::use' action to an external handler PLAQUENS::func00C2; the local script contains no additional logic for using the plaque.
No global flags, parameters, or local persistent state are read or written by these processes in this file; the script's behavior is limited to showing text and delegating U8P_PLAQUEEW::use.
notable_control_flow: U8P_PLAQUEEW::look — linear: show a single bark ('plaque ') then suspend/terminate the U8P_PLAQUEEW::look process.
U8P_PLAQUEEW::use — immediate call to PLAQUENS::func00C2 and return; control is handed off to the external routine for substantive behavior.
open_questions: What exact behavior PLAQUENS::func00C2 implements when the plaque is used (trigger event, menu, state change, animation, etc.) — not present in this file.
Whether the displayed string 'plaque ' intentionally contains a trailing space or if that is an artifact of extraction.
Whether the 'suspend' in the U8P_PLAQUEEW::look process also advances any game state or only pauses for player input (inferred as a read/pause with medium confidence).
